title: Will Machines Take Your Job?
source: AI生成·待校 · 经济与社会
intro: AI 会抢走你的饭碗吗？历史给答案。
paragraphs: Throughout history, technological advances have sparked fears of mass unemployment. The Luddites in 19th-century England destroyed machines, believing they would steal their livelihoods. Yet, each wave of innovation eventually created more jobs than it eliminated. Today, artificial intelligence (AI) is raising similar concerns. Will it replace human workers on a large scale, or will it generate new opportunities?

AI excels at automating routine tasks, such as data entry, assembly line work, and even some aspects of legal analysis. This efficiency can displace workers in predictable roles. However, history shows that automation also creates demand for new skills. For instance, the rise of the internet gave birth to jobs like web developer and social media manager—roles that were unimaginable a few decades ago.

Critics argue that AI is different. Unlike previous technologies, it can perform cognitive tasks—writing, coding, even diagnosing diseases. This could threaten white-collar jobs, not just blue-collar ones. Moreover, the pace of change is faster, leaving little time for workers to retrain. Without proper policies, the gap between the skilled and unskilled may widen, leading to social instability.

Nevertheless, AI is not inherently destructive. It can augment human capabilities, enabling workers to focus on creative and interpersonal tasks. The key is adaptation: investing in education, lifelong learning, and social safety nets. As with past revolutions, those who embrace change will thrive. The future of work depends not on machines, but on how we choose to integrate them into society.
cn: 纵观历史，技术进步曾引发大规模失业的恐慌。19世纪英国的卢德分子捣毁机器，认为它们会夺走生计。然而，每一次创新浪潮最终创造的就业岗位都多于其淘汰的。如今，人工智能引发了类似的担忧。它会在大规模上取代人类工作者，还是会产生新的机会？
人工智能擅长自动化常规任务，例如数据录入、流水线工作，甚至法律分析的某些方面。这种效率可能取代从事可预测角色的人。然而，历史表明，自动化也创造了对新技能的需求。例如，互联网的兴起催生了网页开发者、社交媒体经理等职业——这些角色在几十年前难以想象。
批评者认为人工智能有所不同。与以往技术不同，它能执行认知任务——写作、编程，甚至诊断疾病。这可能威胁白领工作，而不仅仅是蓝领工作。此外，变革速度更快，留给工人再培训的时间很少。如果没有恰当的政策，技能熟练者与不熟练者之间的差距可能扩大，导致社会不稳定。
尽管如此，人工智能并非天生具有破坏性。它可以增强人类能力，使工作者专注于创造性和人际交往任务。关键在于适应：投资教育、终身学习和社会安全网。与过去的革命一样，拥抱变化的人将蓬勃发展。工作的未来不取决于机器，而是取决于我们选择如何将它们融入社会。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: The Luddites were right to destroy machines.
Historical patterns suggest AI may create new jobs despite fears.
AI will inevitably cause mass unemployment.
questions.explain: 主旨题。文章开篇引出技术焦虑，接着讨论AI的替代与创造，最后强调适应的重要性。全文核心论点是：尽管有担忧，但历史表明AI可能创造新就业。

questions.q: According to paragraph 2, what is an example of new jobs created by technological advancement?
questions.explain: 细节题。第二段末句提到互联网催生了网页开发者等职业。

questions.q: What can be inferred from the critics' argument in paragraph 3?
options: Previous technologies did not affect cognitive tasks.
The pace of change allows ample time for retraining.
AI may widen the skills gap.
questions.explain: 推断题。第三段批评者指出AI速度快，可能导致技能差距扩大。

questions.q: What does the author suggest is crucial for the future of work in the last paragraph?
options: Adaptation through education and lifelong learning
questions.explain: 语义题。最后一段明确说「关键在于适应：投资教育、终身学习和社会安全网」。
